generated_comment: Arre beta, office mein kaam sabke liye barabar hona chahiye. Aaj kal ladkiyon ko period leave, phir maternity leave, phir bachcha sambhalne ke naam par alag chhutti milti hai, toh company bhi sochegi na kaun zyada dependable hai. Bura mat mano, par aadmi log ko aisa kuchh nahin milta, phir unke saath thoda anyay sa lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biological and caregiving needs as making them less dependable workers and as unfair special treatment compared to men. It encodes fairness/reverse-discrimination framing and maternity/menstrual leave bias in a casual, concern-for-equality tone.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 62. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 21 just told you and the family over dinner that she wants to start going to the gym in the morning before college. The nearest good gym is a 15 minute walk from the house and she would be going at 6am. You are the first one to respond to this news. You are telling Ritu why this is not a good idea and what can happen to girls who are out alone in the early morning and what she should do instead and what happened to a girl in your neighbourhood three years ago who used to go out early.
